titel: Increased focus on sexual offenses, fraud targeting the elderly, and crimes motivated by hatred based on gender.
html:
In the proposal, the government suggests several amendments to the law aimed at strengthening the criminal protection against sexual offenses, fraud targeting the elderly, and crimes motivated by hatred based on gender. The proposals include the following:

  • Anyone who engages in sexual intercourse or another sexual act with a child under 18 years of age and improperly takes advantage of the fact that the child, due to factors such as mental health issues, disability, or the perpetrator's authority, has a diminished ability to protect their sexual integrity, should be convicted of rape of a minor and sexual abuse of a minor.
  • The term "purchase of sexual services" will be changed to "purchase of sexual acts," and the scope of the criminal offense and the related offense of prostitution will be expanded to include acts performed remotely, i.e., without physical contact.
  • Gender will be added to the provision concerning increased penalties for crimes committed with hate crime motives.
  • More cases of fraud will be classified as serious offenses and subject to harsher penalties, with particular consideration given to whether the perpetrator has callously exploited the victim's vulnerable or exposed position, or whether the act was part of criminal activity carried out in an organized or systematic manner.

The proposed amendments are scheduled to come into effect on July 1, 2025.

Proposed Resolution of the Parliament

Proposal 2024/25:124

Government Proposal: The Riksdag (Swedish Parliament) adopts the government's proposal to amend the Penal Code. 3

Proposal 2024/25:124 2 Proposed Amendment to the Penal Code

Hereby, it is stipulated that sections 6, 4, 6, 9, 11, 12, and 15 of Chapter 6, sections 3 and 3c of Chapter 9, and section 29 of Chapter 29 of the Penal Code shall have the following wording:

Current Wording Proposed Wording

Chapter 6. Section 4, paragraph 1: Whoever, with a child under the age of fifteen, engages in vaginal, anal, or oral intercourse, or another sexual act that, considering the seriousness of the offense, is comparable to intercourse, shall be sentenced for rape of a minor to imprisonment for a minimum of three and a maximum of six years. The same applies to anyone who improperly coerces the child to perform or endure such an act.

Section 4, paragraph 1: The first paragraph also applies to anyone who commits an act as described therein against a child who has turned fifteen but not eighteen and who is a child of the perpetrator or who is under the care or upbringing of, or has a similar relationship to, the perpetrator, or for whose care or supervision the perpetrator is responsible based on a decision by a government agency.

  1. against a child who has turned fifteen but not eighteen.

a) who is a child of the perpetrator. b) who is under the care or upbringing of, or has a similar relationship to, the perpetrator. c) for whose care or supervision the perpetrator is responsible based on a decision by a government agency.

  1. if the perpetrator, in a case other than those mentioned in 1, improperly takes advantage of the fact that a child under the age of eighteen has a reduced ability to protect their sexual integrity due to mental illness, disability, substance abuse, the perpetrator's authority, or otherwise, considering the circumstances, has a reduced ability to protect their sexual integrity.

If an offense described in the first or second paragraph is serious, the offender shall be sentenced for aggravated rape of a minor to imprisonment for a minimum of five and a maximum of ten years. When assessing whether the offense is serious, particular consideration shall be given to whether the perpetrator used violence or threats of criminal acts, or whether more than one person committed the act against the child, or participated in the abuse in other ways, or whether the perpetrator, considering the method used or the child's age, has shown particular recklessness or brutality.

Section 6, paragraph 2: Whoever, with a child under the age of fifteen or with a child who has turned fifteen but not eighteen, and where the circumstances are such as referred to in paragraph 2 of section 4, engages in a sexual act other than those referred to in sections 4 and 5, shall be sentenced for sexual abuse of a minor to imprisonment for a minimum of six months and a maximum of two years. The same applies to anyone who improperly coerces the child to perform or endure such an act. If the offense is less serious, imprisonment shall be a maximum of one year. If the offense is serious, the offender shall be sentenced for aggravated sexual abuse of a minor to imprisonment for a minimum of one year and six months and a maximum of six years. Particular consideration shall be given to whether the perpetrator is related to the child or otherwise exploits their position or abuses a particular trust, or whether more than one person committed the act against the child, or participated in the abuse in other ways, or whether the offense, considering the method used or the child's age, constitutes a reckless exploitation of the child.

Section 9, paragraph 3: Whoever, in a case other than those mentioned previously in this chapter, coerces a child who has not turned eighteen to perform or endure a sexual act for compensation, shall be sentenced for exploiting a child through the purchase of a sexual act to imprisonment for a minimum of six months and a maximum of four years. If the offense is less serious, imprisonment shall be a maximum of one year. What is stated in the first and second paragraphs also applies if the compensation has been promised or given by someone else.

Section 11: Whoever, in a case other than those mentioned previously in this chapter, obtains a temporary sexual relationship for compensation, shall be sentenced for purchasing sexual services to imprisonment for a maximum of one year.
tldr: The government is proposing new laws to provide stronger protection against child sexual abuse, fraud targeting the elderly, and hate crimes. These proposals include stricter penalties for certain offenses and are scheduled to come into effect on July 1, 2025.
lattlast:
The government wants to amend the Penal Code, which is the law that defines crimes and their corresponding punishments. A key proposal is to better protect children up to the age of 18. If an adult exploits a child who is vulnerable and unable to defend themselves, for example, due to mental health issues or a disability, it should be classified as rape of a minor or sexual abuse of a minor.

It will also become illegal to purchase sexual acts, even if it occurs online without physical contact. Previously, the law primarily applied when the interaction was physical. Furthermore, the government wants to impose harsher penalties if a crime is committed due to hatred of someone's gender. This is known as a hate crime, and it means that the perpetrator is punished more severely when the motive is prejudice.

To protect elderly individuals, the government wants to increase the penalties for fraud. If a person commits fraud and exploits someone in a vulnerable position, or if the crime is part of an organized criminal group, it should be considered a serious offense, leading to a longer prison sentence.

All of these changes are proposals that the government is sending to the Parliament for consideration. If the Parliament approves them, the new rules will come into effect throughout Sweden on July 1, 2025.
